target.name: Alef Aeronautics
target.founder: Jim Dukhovny, Constantine Kisly, Pavel Markin, Oleg Petrov

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: REGENT Craft
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series A), post-money $270M

data.narrative:
Alef Aeronautics is a Series A-stage transportation company that has raised $30M+ to date. The relevant peer universe comprises eight private Transportation companies; however, a sufficiently deep peer median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Transportation cohort (only one same-stage peer identified against a minimum threshold of three required datapoints).

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary valuation anchor. Using total raised ($30M) as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-subsequent-stage step-ups yields an implied valuation of $60M. This represents a standard capital progression assumption absent company-specific performance metrics.

The triangulated range is $51M (low), $60M (median), and $69M (high), anchored to the single applicable method. Key uncertainties limiting precision include the absence of tighter peer benchmarking data and reliance on stage-based multipliers rather than revenue, unit economics, or technology readiness milestones. Any valuation within this band should be stress-tested against Alef's specific capitalization structure, burn rate, and product development timeline relative to Transportation peers like Rivian and REGENT Craft, though direct comparability remains limited by stage and segment heterogeneity.
